creator: jgibbons@protogate.com
text: For me this issue occurs immediately when trying to boot from a CD, even without any hard disk present.  This is on a physical machine, not a VM, and with the hard disk disconnected.  I have tried FreeBSD-12.0-STABLE-i386-20190207-r343863-bootonly.iso, FreeBSD-12.0-STABLE-amd64-20190207-r343863-bootonly.iso, and FreeBSD-13.0-CURRENT-i386-20190207-r343862-bootonly.iso, and all 3 result in the loader stopping at the "OK" prompt after the same "LUA ERROR: cannot open /boot/lua/loader.lua: no such file or directory." error (despite the fact that that file is present in each of those CDs) -- and I am unable to traverse the CDROM filesystem from the loader prompt.  I have also tried FreeBSD-11.2-STABLE-i386-20190207-r343863-bootonly.iso, which works perfectly.
